revision.text:
[[ja:厳島神社 (会津若松市)]] {{infobox religious building | name = Itsukushima Shrine (Aizuwakamatsu) | native_name = 厳島神社 | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|qid=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} | image = [[File:厳島神社（会津若松市）.jpg|250px]]
Haiden | address = {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|qid=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}{{ill|Aizuwakamatsu|en|Aizuwakamatsu|qid=Q237699|lt=Aizuwakamatsu|1=Aizuwakamatsu}}一箕町八幡字辨天下甲1405 | location = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|lt_ja=市杵島姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} | shrine_Rank = Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} | founded = Founded during the {{ill|Eitoku|en|Eitoku|qid=Q1145805|lt=Eitoku|1=Eitoku}} period ({{ill|1381|en|1381|qid=Q6331|lt=1381|1=1381}}-{{ill|1383|en|1383|qid=Q6341|lt=1383|1=1383}}) | festival = | location map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Itsukushima Shrine (厳島神社)|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Itsukushima Shrine''' (厳島神社) is a Shinto shrine in {{ill|Aizuwakamatsu|en|Aizuwakamatsu|qid=Q237699|lt=Aizuwakamatsu|1=Aizuwakamatsu}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|qid=Q71707|lt=Fukushima|1=Fukushima Prefecture}} . It was formerly known as '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社) and was constructed during the {{ill|Eitoku|en|Eitoku|qid=Q1145805|lt=Eitoku|1=Eitoku}} period by three local powerful families—Ishizuka, Ishibe, and Dōke. The name Itsukushima Shrine was adopted in {{ill|1868|en|1868|qid=Q7717|lt=1868|1=1868}} (the first year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} era) following the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri Rei|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri Rei|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離|1=Shinbutsu bunri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1970535}}. The {{ill|Bettō|en|Bettō|qid=Q2900274|lt=Bettō|1=Bettō}} is {{ill|Shōsōji|ja|正宗寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōsōji|1=Shōsō-ji temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q48762456}}. Its {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qid=Q712534|lt=Former Shrine Rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} is {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}. == Overview == Because the deity {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|lt_ja=市杵島姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} had syncretized with {{ill|Benzaiten|en|Benzaiten|qid=Q818468|qid=Q818468|lt=Benzaiten|1=Benzaiten}}, the mountain on which the shrine stands, {{ill|Iimori Mountain|en|Iimori Mountain|qid=Q11666635|lt=Iimoriyama|1=Iimori Mountain}}, is also known as Benten Mountain (辯天山). In {{ill|1700|en|1700|qid=Q6813|lt=1700|1=1700}} (the 13th year of the {{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|qid=Q1325001|lt=Genroku|1=Genroku}} era), 580 ken surrounding Iimoriyama were donated by the lord of the {{ill|Aizu Domain|1=Aizu Domain|2=ja|3=会津藩|ja|Aizu Domain|1=Aizu Domain|2=ja|3=会津藩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizu Domain|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q4389944}}, {{ill|Matsudaira Masakata|en|Matsudaira Masakata|qid=Q11239143|lt=Matsudaira Masayori|1=Matsudaira Masakata}}, and a {{ill|Torii|en|Torii|qid=Q234731|lt=Torii|1=Torii}}, a {{ill|Nio Gate|ja|仁王門|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nio Gate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金剛力士|1=Nio (Buddhism)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q539715}}, and a bronze {{ill|Daibutsu|en|Daibutsu|qid=Q1051606|lt=Great Buddha|1=Daibutsu}} were erected. During the Meiji period the Great Buddha was relocated to Amida-ji in {{ill|Nanukamachi Station|en|Nanukamachi Station|qid=Q6964422|lt=Nanokamachi|1=Nanukamachi Station}}, although the torii remains in place. Additionally, in {{ill|1796|en|1796|qid=Q7818|lt=1796|1=1796}} (the 8th year of the {{ill|Kansei|en|Kansei|qid=Q1195131|lt=Kansei|1=Kansei}} era), the {{ill|Eirado|ja|栄螺堂|lt=Former Shōsōji Three‐story Hall|lt_ja=旧正宗寺三匝堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eirado|1=sazae-dō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q125246073}} was built and still stands within the shrine grounds. == Access == * From in front of {{ill|Japan Railways Group|en|Japan Railways Group|qid=Q864495|lt=JR|1=Japan Railways Group}} {{ill|Aizu-Wakamatsu Station|en|Aizu-Wakamatsu Station|qid=Q4699462|lt=Aizuwakamatsu Station|1=Aizu-Wakamatsu Station}}, take the {{ill|City Loop Bus|ja|まちなか周遊バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/City Loop Bus|1=Machinaka Shūyū Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11278356}} "Akabee" or the bus via Nakajima-machi bound for Iai/Matsunaga Housing Complex, and alight at "Iimoriyama-shita" (飯盛山下). {{Shinto Navigation}} {{shinto-stub}} [[sb:厳島神社]] [[ja:厳島神社 (会津若松市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11410135}} [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Aizuwakamatsu]] [[Category:Itsukushima shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
